{{< include latex-macros/macros.qmd >}}

::: {#exr-prac-score-zero}
#### Score at the MLE (adapted from @dobson4e, Chapter 3)

Let $X_1, \ldots, X_n \siid \Pois(\rate)$.

**(a)** Write the log-likelihood $\ell(\rate; \vx)$.

**(b)** Derive the score function $\ell'(\rate; \vx)$.

**(c)** Show that $\hat\rate_{ML} = \bar{x}$,
and verify that the score equals zero at the MLE.

**(d)** Provide an intuitive interpretation:
why does the score being zero at $\hat\rate_{ML}$ make sense?
:::

::: {.solution}

**(a)**

$$
\ba
\ell(\rate; \vx)
&= \logf{\prod_{i=1}^n \frac{\rate^{x_i} e^{-\rate}}{x_i!}}
\\
&= \sum_{i=1}^n \paren{x_i \logf{\rate} - \rate - \logf{x_i!}}
\\
&= \paren{\sum_{i=1}^n x_i}\logf{\rate} - n\rate - \sum_{i=1}^n \logf{x_i!}
\ea
$$

**(b)**

$$
\ba
\ell'(\rate; \vx)
&= \deriv{\rate}\sb{\paren{\sum_{i=1}^n x_i}\logf{\rate} - n\rate}
\\
&= \frac{\sum_{i=1}^n x_i}{\rate} - n
\\
&= \frac{n\bar{x}}{\rate} - n
\ea
$$

**(c)**

Setting $\ell'(\rate; \vx) = 0$:

$$
\ba
\frac{n\bar{x}}{\rate} &= n
\\
\hat\rate_{ML} &= \bar{x}
\ea
$$

If $\bar{x} > 0$
(equivalently,
at least one $x_i > 0$),
then

$$
\ba
\ell'(\bar{x}; \vx)
&= \frac{n\bar{x}}{\bar{x}} - n
\\
&= n - n
\\
&= 0.
\ea
$$

If instead all $x_i = 0$,
then $\bar{x} = 0$ and $\hat\rate_{ML} = 0$ is a boundary value.
In that case,
the score formula above is not defined at $\rate = 0$,
so the usual interior verification
$\ell'(\hat\rate_{ML}; \vx) = 0$
does not apply.

**(d)**

The score measures the rate of change of the log-likelihood.
When it equals zero,
increasing or decreasing $\rate$ slightly would not improve the fit;
we are at a "flat" point.
Intuitively,
$\hat\rate_{ML} = \bar{x}$ is the value of $\rate$ that makes
the expected count per observation ($\rate$) exactly equal to
the observed average count ($\bar{x}$),
the best possible match between model and data.

:::
